content.full_text:
June 10, 1986


Dale F. Hardeman
7909 Fourth Place
Downey, CA  90241
                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             FPPC No. A-86-174

Dear Mr. Hardeman:

     Thank you for your letter of May 13, 1986.  The facts as
presented in your attached letter regarding our previous
conversation are correct.

     Government Code Section 82015 defines the term contribution to
include "... Notwithstanding the foregoing definition of
"contribution," the term does not include volunteer personal
services or payments made by any individual for his or her own
travel expenses if such payments are made voluntarily without any
understanding or agreement that they shall be, directly or
indirectly, repaid to him or her."

     Please do not hesitate to contact me if you need additional
assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

MAK:kt
Attachment




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

DALE F. HARDEMAN
7909 4th Place
Downey, CA 90241

May 13, 1986                                                    10:20 AM

Fair Political Practices Commission
Ms Mary Ann Kvasager
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE:  Request for opinion regarding reportable non-monetary
     contributions to "Friends of Roy Paul Committee", City
     of Downey Council, District 2 election.


Dear Ms Kvasager,

     Thank you for the opportunity to speak with you
regarding the above captioned matter.

     In our conversation, I indicated to you that up until
April 30, 1986, I was a legislative aid to Assemblyman Wayne
Grisham and that I resigned to take a partnership interest in
Target Data Services, a firm which sells mailing lists and
labels, conducts public opinion surveys, and performs data
processing tasks for political candidates and corporate
marketing programs.

     I also indicated to you that Target Data Services has
contracted with Downey City Council candidate Roy Paul for
the sale of above services and that Target Data provides
these services at fair market value.

     It was also indicated that aside from my firm's
services, I volunteer my personal time to occasionally meet
with Mr. Paul's campaign manager and volunteers and to walk
precincts when possible.

     You indicated to me that, in your opinion, if I was not
going to be compensated for my individual services, aside
from that of the Target Data Services, it was not necessary
for Mr. Paul to report my volunteer services as non-monetary
contributions to his campaign and that, based on the
information provided, you could not discern any violation of
Fair Political Practices reporting laws.

FPPC, Mary Ann Kvasager, Page 2.

===================================================================

I would appreciate your providing a signature of
confirmation as to the above conversation and opinion.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Sincerely,

[signature]
DALE F. HARDEMAN

CC: Roy Paul
    File

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 23, 1986


Dale F. Hardeman
7909 - 4th Place
Downey, Ca  90241

Re:  86-174

Dear Mr. Hardeman:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on May 19, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Joanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

( ) JOHN K. ( ) KATHY ( ) MARY ANN

( ) LYNN ( ) JOHN M. ( ) CARLA

( ) JAY ( ) ROGER ( ) HELEN

( ) OTHER:

[ ]TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW

[]CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: A-86-174

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [ ]

CALLER DALE F HARDEMAN

REPRESENTING

PHONE NUMBER:

QUESTION: IS PERSONAL TIME VOLUNTEERED
TO A CANDIDATE'S CAMPAIGN
CONSIDERED A CONTRIBUTION

ANSWER: VOLUNTEER PERSONAL TIME SERVICES
OR PAYMENTS ARE NOT CONTRIBUTIONS
IF SUCH PAYMENT ARE MADE VOLUNTARILY
WITH OUR ANY UNDERSTANDING OR AGREEMENT
THAT THE PERSON WILL BE DIRECTLY
OR INDIRECTLY REPAID

FROM: KWASAGER

DATE: 6/10/86
